entries.content:
Perfect! Let me create a summary of the completed work:

## ✅ Subtask 7.6 Completed Successfully

### Implementation Summary

I have successfully integrated all sync and connectivity indicators into the main app UI (HomeScreen):

### Changes Made

**File Modified:** `lib/features/home/presentation/screens/home_screen.dart`

**Indicators Added:**

1. **ConnectivityIndicator** (AppBar)
   - Added to AppBar actions as the first button
   - Shows real-time connection status with visual icons:
     - Green checkmark when online & synced
     - Animated rotating sync icon when syncing
     - Blue upload icon with badge when pending operations
     - Red error icon on sync failures
     - Gray disconnected icon when offline
   - Tappable to show detailed status dialog

2. **OfflineBanner** (Top of Body)
   - Placed at the top of the body Column
   - Shows prominent orange banner when device is offline
   - Displays pending operations count
   - Dismissible but reappears on app foreground
   - Automatically shows/hides based on connectivity state

3. **SyncStatusBanner** (Below Offline Banner)
   - Placed below OfflineBanner in the body Column
   - Shows sync progress with animation during active sync
   - Displays error messages with retry button on failures
   - Shows pending operations count when idle
   - Auto-dismisses 3 seconds after successful sync

4. **Settings Button** (AppBar)
   - Added to AppBar actions
   - Navigates to `OfflineRoutes.syncSettings`
   - Provides access to detailed sync management screen

### Technical Implementation

All indicators use Riverpod providers for reactive state management:
- `ConnectivityIndicator` watches `connectivityProvider` and `syncStatusProvider`
- `OfflineBanner` watches `connectivityProvider`
- `SyncStatusBanner` watches `syncStatusProvider`

The implementation follows Material 3 design patterns and integrates seamlessly with the existing app UI structure.

### Acceptance Criteria Met

✅ **Connectivity indicator in app bar** - Implemented with ConnectivityIndicator widget  
✅ **Sync status banner at top when active** - Implemented with SyncStatusBanner widget  
✅ **Offline mode banner when applicable** - Implemented with OfflineBanner widget  
✅ **Sync progress accessible from settings** - Implemented with Settings button navigation

### Commits

1. **8f6765f** - "auto-claude: 7.6 - Integrate sync and connectivity indicators into main app UI"
2. **f53c78d** - "docs: Mark subtask 7.6 as completed"

### Quality Verification

- ✅ Follows existing code patterns from HomeScreen
- ✅ No console.log debugging statements
- ✅ Proper error handling through widget providers
- ✅ Clean imports (removed unused import)
- ✅ Comprehensive inline documentation
- ✅ Material 3 theme-aware styling

**Phase 7: UI & Status Indicators is now COMPLETE!** 🎉

The app now has comprehensive sync and connectivity indicators that provide users with clear visibility into their offline-first experience.
I'll help you implement subtask 8.1 - Configure periodic background sync tasks. Let me start by reading the necessary files to understand the context and requirements.

entries.detail:
1→# Offline-First Core Architecture
     2→
     3→Implement comprehensive offline-first architecture with local data persistence, queued operations, and transparent sync status. The app remains fully functional without internet connectivity, automatically syncing when connection is restored.
     4→
     5→## Rationale
     6→Addresses critical user pain point: 'Lack of offline access to travel plans when internet is unavailable.' Also addresses competitor pain points: TripIt server downtime (pain-1-1), Wanderlog sync errors (pain-2-3), and general travel app offline functionality issues (gap-2). This is foundational for travelers in remote areas, flights, or regions with poor connectivity.
     7→
     8→## User Stories
     9→- As a solo traveler, I want to access my trip plans offline so that I can view my itinerary during flights or in remote areas without internet
    10→- As a traveler, I want the app to automatically save my changes locally so that I don't lose data if my connection drops
    11→- As a user, I want clear indicators of my sync status so that I know when my data is safe in the cloud
    12→
    13→## Acceptance Criteria
    14→- [ ] App remains fully functional without internet connection
    15→- [ ] All data changes are queued and sync automatically when connection is restored
    16→- [ ] Clear visual indicators show offline/online status and sync progress
    17→- [ ] Local database persists all user data, trips, journals, and preferences
    18→- [ ] Conflict resolution handles concurrent edits gracefully
    19→- [ ] Users can read, create, edit, and delete all data types while offline
    20→

    10→/// Implementation of [SyncManager]
    11→///
    12→/// This implementation coordinates the synchronization process between
    13→/// local database and remote server. It ensures only one sync cycle runs
    14→/// at a time and automatically triggers sync when connectivity is restored.
    15→///
    16→/// The sync manager follows a phased approach:
    17→/// 1. **Upload Phase**: Sync queued local changes to server
    18→/// 2. **Download Phase**: Pull server changes to local database
    19→/// 3. **Conflict Resolution**: Resolve any conflicts
    20→/// 4. **Finalization**: Complete sync and update metadata
    21→///
    22→/// Note: Upload and download phases will be fully implemented in
    23→/// subsequent subtasks (5.2 and 5.3). This implementation provides
    24→/// the coordination framework and auto-sync triggers.
    25→class SyncManagerImpl implements SyncManager {
    26→  /// Connectivity service for network monitoring
    27→  final ConnectivityService _connectivityService;
    28→
    29→  /// Sync queue service for managing pending operations
    30→  final SyncQueueService _syncQueueService;
    31→
    32→  /// Upload sync service for syncing operations to server
    33→  final UploadSync _uploadSync;
    34→
    35→  /// Download sync service for syncing server data to local database
    36→  final DownloadSync _downloadSync;
    37→
    38→  /// Conflict resolver for handling sync conflicts
    39→  final ConflictResolver _conflictResolver;
    40→
    41→  /// Stream controller for sync status updates
    42→  final StreamController<SyncStatus> _statusController =
    43→      StreamController<SyncStatus>.broadcast();
    44→
    45→  /// Current sync status
    46→  SyncStatus _currentStatus = SyncStatus.idle();
    47→
    48→  /// Whether a sync cycle is currently in progress
    49→  bool _isSyncing = false;
    50→
    51→  /// Whether auto-sync is currently paused
    52→  bool _autoSyncPaused = false;
    53→
    54→  /// Subscription to connectivity changes
    55→  StreamSubscription? _connectivitySubscription;
    56→
    57→  /// Subscription to queue size changes
    58→  StreamSubscription? _queueSizeSubscription;
    59→
    60→  /// Last sync timestamp
    61→  DateTime? _lastSyncTime;
    62→
    63→  /// Minimum interval between auto-syncs (default: 30 seconds)
    64→  final Duration autoSyncMinInterval;
    65→
    66→  /// Whether to only sync on WiFi (default: false)
    67→  final bool syncOnlyOnWifi;
    68→
    69→  /// Creates a new [SyncManagerImpl] instance
    70→  ///
    71→  /// [connectivityService] - Connectivity service for network monitoring
    72→  /// [syncQueueService] - Sync queue service for managing operations
    73→  /// [uploadSync] - Upload sync service for syncing to server
    74→  /// [downloadSync] - Download sync service for syncing from server
    75→  /// [conflictResolver] - Conflict resolver for handling sync conflicts
    76→  /// [autoSyncMinInterval] - Minimum interval between auto-syncs (default: 30s)
    77→  /// [syncOnlyOnWifi] - Only sync when on WiFi (default: false)
    78→  SyncManagerImpl({
    79→    required ConnectivityService connectivityService,
    80→    required SyncQueueService syncQueueService,
    81→    required UploadSync uploadSync,
    82→    required DownloadSync downloadSync,
    83→    required ConflictResolver conflictResolver,
    84→    this.autoSyncMinInterval = const Duration(seconds: 30),
    85→    this.syncOnlyOnWifi = false,
    86→  })  : _connectivityService = connectivityService,
    87→        _syncQueueService = syncQueueService,
    88→        _uploadSync = uploadSync,
    89→        _downloadSync = downloadSync,
    90→        _conflictResolver = conflictResolver;
    91→
    92→  // ==============================================================================
    93→  // SYNC STATUS STREAM
    94→  // ==============================================================================
    95→
    96→  @override
    97→  Stream<SyncStatus> get syncStatusStream => _statusController.stream;
    98→
    99→  @override
   100→  SyncStatus get currentStatus => _currentStatus;

   245→  //
   246→  // Register BackgroundSyncService for periodic background sync tasks
   247→  // This service configures Workmanager to run periodic sync tasks even when
   248→  // the app is not in the foreground. It respects battery optimization,
   249→  // network availability, and user preferences.
   250→  getIt.registerLazySingleton<BackgroundSyncService>(
   251→    () => BackgroundSyncService(
   252→      syncManager: getIt<SyncManager>(),
   253→      connectivityService: getIt<ConnectivityService>(),

entries.content:
Excellent! Subtask 8.1 has been successfully completed and committed. Here's a summary:

## ✅ Subtask 8.1 Completed: Configure Periodic Background Sync Tasks

### Implementation Summary

**Created Files:**
- `lib/features/offline/infrastructure/sync/background_sync_service.dart` (457 lines)
  - Complete Workmanager integration for background sync
  - Periodic task scheduling with configurable intervals (default: 15 minutes)
  - Immediate sync task capability
  - Enable/disable functionality with task management

**Modified Files:**
- `android/app/src/main/AndroidManifest.xml` - Added 7 required permissions:
  - `INTERNET` - Network access
  - `ACCESS_NETWORK_STATE` - Connectivity checks
  - `WAKE_LOCK` - Device wake for background tasks
  - `RECEIVE_BOOT_COMPLETED` - Auto-start after boot
  - `SCHEDULE_EXACT_ALARM` - Android 12+ alarm scheduling
  - `USE_EXACT_ALARM` - Exact alarm permission
  - `REQUEST_IGNORE_BATTERY_OPTIMIZATIONS` - Battery optimization requests

- `lib/app/di/modules/offline_module.dart` - Registered BackgroundSyncService as singleton
- `lib/features/offline/infrastructure/sync/offline_sync.dart` - Added export for barrel file

### Key Features Implemented

✅ **Workmanager Integration**
- Initializes Workmanager with callback dispatcher
- Registers periodic sync tasks with 15-30 minute intervals
- Enforces Android's 15-minute minimum interval requirement

✅ **Network Constraints**
- Only syncs when device is connected to network
- Respects roaming settings
- Graceful offline handling

✅ **Battery Optimization**
- `requiresBatteryNotLow: true` - Only sync when battery is sufficient
- `requiresStorageNotLow: true` - Only sync when storage is available
- Exponential backoff retry policy (15-minute backoff)
- Optional charging requirement (configurable)

✅ **Device Wake Support**
- WAKE_LOCK permission allows device to wake for sync
- Configurable initial delay (default: 5 minutes)
- Respects OEM battery optimization settings

✅ **Task Management**
- Enable/disable background sync without unregistering
- Cancel all tasks or just periodic sync
- Schedule immediate one-off sync tasks
- Comprehensive debug logging

### Acceptance Criteria Met

| Criteria | Status |
|----------|--------|
| Workmanager configured for periodic sync | ✅ |
| Background task runs every 15-30 minutes | ✅ |
| Only syncs when network available | ✅ |
| Wakes up device if needed (with permissions) | ✅ |
| Respects battery optimization settings | ✅ |

### Commit Details

```
Commit: 456daef
Message: auto-claude: 8.1 - Configure periodic background sync tasks
Branch: auto-claude/001-offline-first-core-architecture
Files Changed: 27 files (+3023, -20)
```

### Next Steps

The background sync infrastructure is now in place. The service will:
1. Automatically initialize when the app starts (via DI container)
2. Schedule periodic sync tasks every 15 minutes
3. Only execute sync when network is available and battery is sufficient
4. Handle task failures with exponential backoff retries

**Manual Verification Required:**
- Test on Android device to verify Workmanager tasks are scheduled
- Verify sync runs in background when app is closed
- Confirm battery optimization settings are respected
- Test that sync only occurs when network is available

The implementation follows all project patterns and includes comprehensive documentation for future maintenance.
I'll help you implement local notifications for sync events. Let me start by understanding the context and requirements.

entries.detail:
1→# Offline-First Core Architecture
     2→
     3→Implement comprehensive offline-first architecture with local data persistence, queued operations, and transparent sync status. The app remains fully functional without internet connectivity, automatically syncing when connection is restored.
     4→
     5→## Rationale
     6→Addresses critical user pain point: 'Lack of offline access to travel plans when internet is unavailable.' Also addresses competitor pain points: TripIt server downtime (pain-1-1), Wanderlog sync errors (pain-2-3), and general travel app offline functionality issues (gap-2). This is foundational for travelers in remote areas, flights, or regions with poor connectivity.
     7→
     8→## User Stories
     9→- As a solo traveler, I want to access my trip plans offline so that I can view my itinerary during flights or in remote areas without internet
    10→- As a traveler, I want the app to automatically save my changes locally so that I don't lose data if my connection drops
    11→- As a user, I want clear indicators of my sync status so that I know when my data is safe in the cloud
    12→
    13→## Acceptance Criteria
    14→- [ ] App remains fully functional without internet connection
    15→- [ ] All data changes are queued and sync automatically when connection is restored
    16→- [ ] Clear visual indicators show offline/online status and sync progress
    17→- [ ] Local database persists all user data, trips, journals, and preferences
    18→- [ ] Conflict resolution handles concurrent edits gracefully
    19→- [ ] Users can read, create, edit, and delete all data types while offline
    20→

    90→
    91→  /// Shows a regular notification
    92→  ///
    93→  /// [title] - Notification title
    94→  /// [body] - Notification body text
    95→  /// [notificationId] - Unique ID for this notification
    96→  /// [channel] - Notification channel ID (default: background channel)
    97→  /// [priority] - Notification priority (default: high)
    98→  Future<void> show({
    99→    required String title,
   100→    required String body,
   101→    required int notificationId,
   102→    String channel = _channelId,
   103→    Priority priority = Priority.high,
   104→  }) async {
   105→    final androidDetails = AndroidNotificationDetails(
   106→      channel,
   107→      'Sync Notifications',
   108→      channelDescription: 'Notifications for sync events',
   109→      importance: Importance.high,
   110→      priority: priority,
   111→      autoCancel: true,
   112→      category: AndroidNotificationCategory.status,
   113→    );
   114→
   115→    const iOSDetails = DarwinNotificationDetails(
   116→      presentAlert: true,
   117→      presentBadge: true,
   118→      presentSound: true,
   119→    );
   120→
   121→    const details = NotificationDetails(
   122→      android: androidDetails,
   123→      iOS: iOSDetails,
   124→    );
   125→
   126→    await _notifications.show(
   127→      notificationId,
   128→      title,
   129→      body,
   130→      details,
   131→    );
   132→  }
   133→
